title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
AFT Notice of Change
An AFT Notice of Change (NOC) transaction is a non-financial transaction created by your financial institution that receives an AFT transaction. The NOC transaction informs the Originator of changes to the payment information (e.g., the Transit Number or account number).
Automated Funds Transfers (AFT)
An Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) is a Debit or Credit Transaction. Debit Transactions are known as Pre-authorized Debits (PADs). Credit Transactions are known as Direct Deposits.
Branch Capture
Branch Capture (a form of Remote Deposit Capture) is the process financial institutions use to scan images of cheques, money orders, MICR-encoded Canadian Savings Bonds, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants received on deposit using scanners and capture software in their back office. Images of these items are transmitted to Central 1 for processing.
Branch Number
The Branch Number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number.
Chargeback
A Chargeback is
an item that your financial institution (as the negotiating financial institution) deposits that is subsequently dishonoured by the Drawee financial institution.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications.
Corporate Capture
Corporate Capture, a form of Remote Deposit Capture, is the process financial institution business customers use to scan images of cheques, money orders, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants received on deposit using scanners and capture software from the business customerГўВҖВҷs location. Images of these items are transmitted to Central 1 for processing.
Credit Transaction
A Credit Transaction is a credit payment item exchanged in an AFT file as defined in Payments Canada Standard 005. It is also known as a Direct Deposit.
Debit Transaction
A Debit Transaction is a Pre-authorized Debit (PAD) within the meaning of Payments Canada Rule H1, Pre-Authorized Debits, exchanged in an AFT file as defined in Payments Canada Standard 005.
Direct Deposit
A Direct Deposit is an AFT Credit Transaction such as a payroll deposit.
Drawee
A Drawee is your financial institution to which a debit payment item is addressed. The Drawee is directed to pay the specified amount of money.
Drawer
A Drawer is the person who signs the cheque ordering the Drawee financial institution to pay the specified amount of money stated on it.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
Image File
An Image File is a file that Central 1 provides to financial institutions or their statement service providers that contains images of cleared cheques drawn on customersГўВҖВҷ accounts. These cheque images can be included in customersГўВҖВҷ statements.
Image Printout
An Image Printout is a paper output of an image created by a customer of the Payments Canada.
Incoming Clearing Item
An Incoming Clearing Item is a cheque, draft, or money order that is drawn on your financial institution or customer of your financial institution, deposited by the Payee, and sent through Clearing for debit to the DrawerГўВҖВҷs account.
Institution Number
The Institution Number is a 3-digit number assigned by the Payments Canada that identifies a financial institution (e.g., 809 for BC financial institutions and 828 for Ontario financial institutions). The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number. The Institution Number is sometimes known as the route number.
Member Payee
A Member Payee is a financial institution that is also a Payee.
MICR
Magnetic Ink Character Recognition (MICR) means characters printed with ink that contains particles of a magnetic material that allow for machine recognition.
Mobile Deposit
Mobile Deposits are cheques, money orders, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants that are deposited by customers to their accounts at your financial institution, using an application on a mobile device.
Originating Direct Clearer
The Originating Direct Clearer is the Direct Clearer that delivers AFT files to another Direct Clearer.
Originator
An Originator is the financial institution or business customer that creates an AFT file.
Originator ID
The Originator ID is a 10-digit number that identifies the Originator of AFT transactions or the Originator of returned AFT transactions.
ORS
Online Return System (ORS) is a web-based system that facilitates the return of cheques. It allows users to research deposit items and Incoming Clearing Items and to reinstate cheques. ORS also facilitates the return of AFT Transactions and the creation of AFT Notice of Change transactions.
Payee
The Payee is the person or entity to whom funds are to be paid.
Payor
The Payor is the person or entity whose account is debited.
Pre-Authorized Debit (PAD)
A Pre-Authorized Debit (PAD) is an AFT transaction issued by a Payee or Member Payee that is drawn on an account of a Payor (e.g., a mortgage payment, strata payment). See Debit Transaction.
Processing Direct Clearer
The Processing Direct Clearer is the Direct Clearer that receives AFT transactions from an Originating Direct Clearer.
Qualified Item
Qualified Items are cheques or drafts that meet Payments Canada standards and are MICR encoded with the Transit Number and dollar value. See Payments Canada, Standard 006, Specifications for MICR-Encoded Payment Items.
Research User
A Research User is an ORS user who is authorized to search for financial institution cheques, perform deposit research on deposit slips and cheques on deposit, and view, print, and save images of deposit items and cheques.
Return Replacement Document
A Return Replacement Document (RRD) is a form of an Image Printout that meets the specification in Payments Canada Standard 013, Return Replacement Document Design Standard, and which can be used for the purpose of return in accordance with Payments Canada Rule A10, Images, Image Printouts and Return Replacement Documents.
Return User
A Return User is an ORS user who has all the permissions of a Research User and is authorized to search and view AFT transactions, return cheques and AFT transactions, create AFT Notice of Change transactions, reinstate incorrect information on cheques including the Transit Number, account number, and cheque number.
Script User
A Script User is an ORS user who is authorized to upload Cheque Return (RI) files, AFT Return (RTN) files, and AFT Notice of Change (NOC) files to ORS manually or via Central 1ГўВҖВҷs FTP Server.
Transit Number
The Transit Number is the Institution Number together with the Branch Number (e.g., 80912010).
Upload User
An Upload User is an ORS user who has all the permissions of a Return User and is authorized to upload the Cheque Return (RI) files, AFT Return (RTN) files, and AFT Notice of Change (NOC) files manually to ORS.
Unqualified Item
Unqualified Items are cheques or drafts that donГўВҖВҷt meet Payments Canada standards for a Qualified Item or cannot be imaged by Central 1ГўВҖВҷs imaging equipment (e.g., the item is too damaged). Central 1 determines whether an item is qualified or unqualified.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users.
User Management Security Officer
User Management Security Officers use the User Management to add, modify, and delete Client Centre Users and to modify and delete other User Management Security Officers. User Management Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
Incoming Clearing Items
Question 1
I received an Unqualified/Return Cheque Adjustment Slip from CentralВ 1 for an Unqualified Item. I cannot find this cheque on ORS. Is this expected?
Answer
Yes. If Central 1 receives an Unqualified Item and identifies the financial institution on which the item is drawn, Central 1 creates an Unqualified/Return Cheque Adjustment Slip and debits the financial institution. The Unqualified Item is included in PDF format on the File and Report Exchange. See the Incoming Clearing Items Part for more information. Since the cheque wasnГўВҖВҷt imaged, it isnГўВҖВҷt available in ORS, your settlement files, or your clearing reports.
Question 2
Can ORS help me check for duplicate items?
Answer
Yes. Select the Suspect Duplicates tab daily.
This function identifies cheques drawn on your financial institution / customer that were deposited multiple times within 180 days. If a duplicate payment is identified, return it on ORS within 90 calendar days of the clearing date.
Question 3
What is meant by ГўВҖВңreinstatingГўВҖВқ a cheque and why would I use this function on ORS?
Answer
If you identify an Incoming Clearing Item that was captured on ORS with the wrong Transit Number, customer account number, or cheque number, you can enter the correct information on ORS. This is called ГўВҖВңreinstating a cheque.ГўВҖВқ Use of this function is optional. The benefits of using this function are
(i) the ORS database is correct, which facilitates future inquires, and
(ii) the correct information will be included in the Image File for customer statements (if applicable).
Cheque Returns
Question 4
Is it okay to return a cheque on ORS if it requires a declaration?
Answer
Yes. Certain return reason codes require that the customer signs a declaration form (e.g., intended Payee not paid, material alteration if returned after one business day, and forged endorsement). Retain the declaration and a copy of the cheque for 12 months because you must be able to provide a copy of the declaration to the PayeeГўВҖВҷs financial institution upon request.
Notice of Change Transactions
Question 5
What is a Notice of Change transaction?
Answer
If your financial institution receives an incoming AFT transaction for credit or debit to a customer but the Transit Number or account number is incorrect, use ORS to forward the customerГўВҖВҷs correct information to the Originating Direct Clearer so that the Originator can correct the information. This will ensure that subsequent transactions are sent with the correct information which allows the transactions to be posted more quickly to the recipientГўВҖВҷs account. This is known as a Notice of Change transaction and involves the exchange of information only.
END
